content_en:
In the tenth year of the Hijra (632 CE), the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ performed what would prove to be his only complete Hajj — the Farewell Pilgrimage (Hajjat al-Wada'), so named both because he repeatedly said farewell during it and because those who were present somehow sensed they were witnessing a final gathering. The entire Muslim world came — estimates range from ninety to one hundred and forty thousand pilgrims, an unprecedented gathering for any event in Arabia.

The Prophet ﷺ departed Medina on the 25th of Dhul-Qa'dah with his wives and the vast throng of Companions. He entered the state of ihram at the Miqat (the designated station for assuming pilgrimage dress) and proceeded to Mecca, performing tawaf (circumambulation) and sa'i (the walking between Safa and Marwa) before heading to Mina, then to the Plain of Arafat for the standing (wuquf) — the central pillar of Hajj — on the 9th of Dhul-Hijjah.

At Arafat, the Prophet ﷺ delivered the Farewell Sermon (Khutbat al-Wada') — one of the most important speeches in human history. Speaking from the back of his camel Qaswa, addressing the vast assembled multitude, his words were repeated by criers to those who could not hear directly. The sermon's themes are extraordinary in their comprehensiveness: the sanctity of blood, property, and honor ('your blood, your property, and your honor are sacred among you, as sacred as this day, in this month, in this city'); the abolition of all pre-Islamic blood feuds; the condemnation of usury (riba); the commandment of kind treatment of women and their rights over men as well as men's rights over them; the equality of all human beings regardless of race or ethnicity ('an Arab has no superiority over a non-Arab, nor a non-Arab over an Arab, nor a white person over a black person, nor a black person over a white person, except through piety'); the preservation of the Book of Allah and the Sunnah of His Prophet ﷺ as the guarantee against going astray.

During the days at Arafat, the final verse of the Quran in terms of legislation descended: 'This day I have perfected for you your religion, completed My favor upon you, and chosen Islam as your religion' (Quran 5:3). When Abu Bakr heard this verse, he wept — understanding, as did many of the scholars among the Companions, that a completed and perfected revelation implied that the Messenger who bore it was nearing the end of his mission.

The Prophet ﷺ completed the remaining rites of Hajj: the sacrifice in Mina, the shaving of the head, the final circumambulation — all performed with meticulous care and with constant instruction to the Companions: 'Take your rites from me, for I do not know if I will perform Hajj after this year.' He was right. He would never perform Hajj again. This pilgrimage — witnessed by the largest gathering in prophetic history — became the preserved template for every Hajj performed by the billion-plus Muslims who have followed since.
content_id:
Pada tahun kesepuluh Hijriah (632 M), Nabi Muhammad ﷺ melaksanakan apa yang akan terbukti sebagai satu-satunya Haji sempurna beliau — Haji Wada' (Haji Perpisahan), dinamakan demikian karena beliau berulang kali mengucapkan selamat tinggal selama pelaksanaannya dan karena mereka yang hadir entah bagaimana merasakan bahwa mereka sedang menyaksikan perkumpulan terakhir. Seluruh dunia Muslim datang — diperkirakan 114.000 hingga 124.000 sahabat.

Dalam Khutbah Wada' di Padang Arafah pada tanggal 9 Dzulhijjah, Nabi ﷺ menyampaikan pidato yang merangkum prinsip-prinsip utama Islam dan menandai berakhirnya misi kenabian beliau. Di antara yang beliau sampaikan:

"Wahai manusia, sesungguhnya darahmu, hartamu, dan kehormatanmu adalah haram atas satu sama lain, seperti haramnya hari ini, di bulan ini, di negeri ini... Wahai manusia, sesungguhnya aku telah meninggalkan kepada kalian sesuatu yang apabila kalian berpegang teguh padanya, kalian tidak akan tersesat: Kitabullah dan Sunnah Nabi-Nya... Wahai manusia, Tuhanmu satu, dan bapakmu satu (Adam). Tidak ada kelebihan orang Arab atas orang non-Arab, tidak pula sebaliknya, tidak ada kelebihan orang berkulit merah atas orang berkulit hitam, tidak pula sebaliknya, kecuali dengan takwa."

Di ujung Arafah, turunlah wahyu terakhir: "Pada hari ini telah Aku sempurnakan agamamu untukmu, dan telah Aku cukupkan nikmat-Ku bagimu, dan telah Aku ridhai Islam sebagai agamamu." (QS. Al-Ma'idah: 3). Ketika Abu Bakr (ra) mendengar ayat ini, ia menangis — karena ia memahami bahwa kesempurnaan agama berarti tugas kenabian hampir selesai.
content_ar:
في العام العاشر من الهجرة (632م) أدى النبي محمد ﷺ حجته الكاملة الوحيدة — حجة الوداع — التي سُمّيت بذلك لأنه ﷺ كان يُكثر من توديع الناس فيها، ولأن من حضرها استشعروا بطريقة ما أنهم يشهدون لقاءً أخيراً. وقد قدم المسلمون من كل أصقاع الجزيرة، وتتراوح التقديرات بين تسعين ومئة وأربعين ألف حاج في تجمّع لم تشهده جزيرة العرب من قبل.

غادر النبي ﷺ المدينة في الخامس والعشرين من ذي القعدة مع أزواجه وجموع الصحابة الغفيرة، فأحرم عند الميقات وطاف وسعى ثم توجه إلى منى ثم إلى عرفات للوقوف في التاسع من ذي الحجة.

وفي عرفة ألقى النبي ﷺ خطبة الوداع، من أعظم الخطب في تاريخ البشرية. وكان يتكلم من على ناقته القصواء مخاطباً الجموع الحاشدة، ويُبلّغ المُبلِّغون من لم تبلغهم الأصوات. وجاءت موضوعاتها شاملة عميقة: حرمة الدماء والأموال والأعراض، وإلغاء كل ثارات الجاهلية، وتحريم الربا، والوصية بالنساء خيراً وتقرير حقوقهن، ووحدة الأمة الإسلامية بلا فرق بين عربي وأعجمي ولا بين أبيض وأسود إلا بالتقوى.

وتُوّجت الحجة بنزول آية: «الْيَوْمَ أَكْمَلْتُ لَكُمْ دِينَكُمْ وَأَتْمَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعْمَتِي وَرَضِيتُ لَكُمُ الْإِسْلَامَ دِينًا» (المائدة: 3). ويُسجّل المباركفوري أن أبا بكر الصديق بكى حين تُليت الآية، كأنه أدرك أن اكتمال الدين قد يكون إيذاناً بوشك الفراق.
